from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a comics character into a fluent single sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to turn a single triple into a phrase.
    # If `include_subject` is False, return only the verb phrase (e.g., "was created by X").
    def _make_phrase(s: str, pred: str, o: str, include_subject: bool = True) -> str:
        o = o.strip('"\'')
        pred = pred.lower()

        def _singular(val: str) -> str:
            if val.endswith('s') and not val.endswith('ss') and ' ' not in val:
                return val[:-1]
            return val

        # Mapping for full‑sentence (subject included) and fragment (subject omitted)
        full = {
            "alternativename": f"{s} is also known as {o}",
            "creator": f"{s} was created by {o}",
            "fullname": f"{s}'s full name is {o}",
            "foundedby": f"{s} was founded by {o}",
            "keyperson": f"{o} is a key person at {s}",
            "city": f"{s} is located in {o}",
            "product": f"{s} produces {o}",
            "lastaired": f"The last episode of {s} aired on {o}",
            "starring": f"{s} stars {o}",
            "firstappearanceinfilm": f"{s} first appeared in the film {o}",
            "voice": f"{o} provides the voice for {s}",
            "distributor": f"{o} distributed {s}",
            "birthplace": f"{s} was born in {o}",
            "nationality": f"{s} is {_singular(o)}",
            "award": f"{s} received the {o}",
            "broadcastedby": f"{s} was broadcasted by {o}",
            "firstaired": f"{s} first aired on {o}",
            "series": f"{s} is part of the series {o}",
            "child": f"{s} has a child named {o}",
        }

        fragment = {
            "alternativename": f"is also known as {o}",
            "creator": f"was created by {o}",
            "fullname": f"'s full name is {o}",
            "foundedby": f"was founded by {o}",
            "keyperson": f"{o} is a key person at {s}",
            "city": f"is located in {o}",
            "product": f"produces {o}",
            "lastaired": f"The last episode aired on {o}",
            "starring": f"stars {o}",
            "firstappearanceinfilm": f"first appeared in the film {o}",
            "voice": f"{o} provides the voice for {s}",
            "distributor": f"{o} distributed {s}",
            "birthplace": f"was born in {o}",
            "nationality": f"is {_singular(o)}",
            "award": f"received the {o}",
            "broadcastedby": f"was broadcasted by {o}",
            "firstaired": f"first aired on {o}",
            "series": f"is part of the series {o}",
            "child": f"has a child named {o}",
        }

        if pred not in full:
            # Fallback to a generic construction
            return f"{s} {pred} {o}" if include_subject else f"{pred} {o}"

        return full[pred] if include_subject else fragment[pred]

    # Preserve input order while grouping by subject
    subject_order: list[str] = []
    subject_map: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = {}  # subject -> list of (predicate, object)
    for triple in triples:
        if triple.subject not in subject_map:
            subject_order.append(triple.subject)
            subject_map[triple.subject] = []
        subject_map[triple.subject].append((triple.predicate, triple.object))

    # Build clauses per subject, using the first predicate as a full phrase and the rest as fragments
    clauses: list[str] = []
    for subj in subject_order:
        pred_objs = subject_map[subj]
        if not pred_objs:
            continue
        # First predicate gets the full phrase (subject included)
        first_pred, first_obj = pred_objs[0]
        clause = _make_phrase(subj, first_pred, first_obj, include_subject=True)
        # Remaining predicates become fragments joined with commas
        if len(pred_objs) > 1:
            fragments = [
                _make_phrase(subj, p, o, include_subject=False)
                for p, o in pred_objs[1:]
            ]
            clause += ", " + ", ".join(fragments)
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into one sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    sentence = ", ".join(clauses)
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
